name: Intro to Statistics
description: Test your basic knowledge of fundamental statistical principles and their applications.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of statistics?
options: To collect, analyze, interpret, present, and organize data
To study the properties of numbers and mathematical structures
To develop new programming languages and algorithms
To conduct experiments in chemistry and physics
questions.explain: Statistics is fundamentally about the systematic handling and interpretation of data to extract meaningful insights.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a population and a sample in statistics?
options: A population is always smaller than a sample
A sample includes all members of a group, while a population is a subset
A population represents the entire group of interest, while a sample is a representative subset
They are interchangeable terms used to describe any collection of data
questions.explain: A population encompasses all individuals or items of interest, whereas a sample is a smaller, manageable subset taken from that population for study.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of descriptive statistics?
options: Predicting the outcome of a future election based on a survey
Calculating the average age of students in a specific class
Estimating the average income of all citizens in a country from a household survey
Determining if a new medical treatment is effective for the general public
questions.explain: Descriptive statistics involve summarizing and describing the characteristics of a dataset, such as calculating an average for a specific group.

questions.question: Inferential statistics primarily deals with:
options: Summarizing data using graphs and tables
Making predictions or generalizations about a population based on a sample
Organizing raw data into meaningful categories without drawing conclusions
Calculating exact values for every member of an entire population
questions.explain: Inferential statistics uses data from a sample to draw conclusions or make predictions about a larger, unobserved population.

questions.question: A variable that can take on a finite or countably infinite number of values, typically obtained by counting, is called a:
options: Continuous variable
Qualitative variable
Discrete variable
Nominal variable
questions.explain: Discrete variables have distinct, separate values, often whole numbers that result from counting.

questions.question: Which type of variable categorizes data without any intrinsic order or ranking?
options: Ordinal variable
Nominal variable
Ratio variable
Interval variable
questions.explain: Nominal variables are used for labels or names to categorize data without any quantitative value or specific order.

questions.question: What does the 'mean' represent in a dataset?
options: The middle value when the data is arranged in order
The most frequently occurring value in the dataset
The sum of all values divided by the number of values
The difference between the highest and lowest values in the dataset
questions.explain: The mean is the arithmetic average, calculated by adding all data points and dividing by the count of those points.

questions.question: The 'median' of a arranged dataset in ascending or descending order defined as:
options: The average of the highest and lowest values
The value that appears most often in the dataset
The middle value in the dataset
The square root of the dataset's variance
questions.explain: The median is the central value of an ordered dataset, dividing it into two equal halves, with half the values above it and half below.

questions.question: What does the 'mode' signify in a dataset?
options: The sum of all data points
The value that occurs with the highest frequency
The value exactly in the center of the dataset
The total spread or range of the dataset
questions.explain: The mode is the value that appears most frequently in a dataset, indicating the most common observation.

questions.question: If a researcher surveys 200 high school students to understand the study habits of all high school students in a city, the 200 students constitute a:
options: Population
Parameter
Statistic
Sample
questions.explain: A sample is a subset of the population chosen for study, representing the larger group from which it was drawn.
